title: NASA Science
description: Discovering the secrets of the universe, searching for life elsewhere, and protecting and improving life on Earth and in space.

items.title: Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides
items.description:
Heavy rains from Tropical Cyclone Maila triggered a deadly landslide in the mountains of East New Britain.

The post Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
items.description:
Description Team members past and present from NASA’s 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission gathered on April 15, 2026, to celebrate 25 years since the spacecraft’s launch, which took place April 7, 2001. For the occasion, the team rolled out a giant global map of Mars created using imagery from Odyssey’s THEMIS (Thermal Emission Imaging System) […]

The post Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: US-Indian Spacecraft Captures Mexico City Subsidence
items.description:
Description A scientist produced this map of land subsidence (sinking) in Mexico City using data from the NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) mission captured between Oct. 25, 2025, and Jan. 17, 2026. The region has been a well-known hot spot of subsidence for decades, and images like this help confirm that NISAR is performing as […]

The post US-Indian Spacecraft Captures Mexico City Subsidence appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Fires Rage in Georgia
items.description:
Firefighters are battling two destructive blazes in the southern part of the state as drought grips the U.S. Southeast.

The post Fires Rage in Georgia appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Curiosity Captures a 360-Degree View at ‘Nevado Sajama’
items.description:
Description NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover captured this 360-degree view of a region filled with low ridges called boxwork formations between Nov. 9 and Dec. 7, 2025 (the 4,714th to 4,741st Martian days, or sols, of the mission). At 1.5 billion pixels, this is one of the largest panoramas Curiosity has ever taken (the rover’s largest […]

The post Curiosity Captures a 360-Degree View at ‘Nevado Sajama’ appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Six Years of Curiosity’s Wheels on the Move
items.description:
Description NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover used its right navigation camera — one of two on the rover’s mast, or head — to capture the images in this timelapse, which spans six years of driving. The images were snapped between Jan. 2, 2020, and March 8, 2026 (the 2,633rd and 4,830th Martian day, or sol, of […]

The post Six Years of Curiosity’s Wheels on the Move appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Snow Is Scarce in the Upper Colorado Basin
items.description:
The mountains of Utah and Colorado are among the areas of the western U.S. that are low on snow and facing water worries in spring 2026.

The post Snow Is Scarce in the Upper Colorado Basin appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Smoke Shrouds Northern Thailand
items.description:
Seasonal fires have darkened skies over Southeast Asia.

The post Smoke Shrouds Northern Thailand appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: A School of Mud Volcano Islands in Azerbaijan
items.description:
The tadpole-shaped islands along the Absheron Peninsula were born by explosive mud volcano eruptions and reshaped by erosion.

The post A School of Mud Volcano Islands in Azerbaijan appeared first on NASA Science.

items.title: Spring Rains Saturate Michigan
items.description:
Above-normal precipitation has swollen rivers and damaged infrastructure statewide.

The post Spring Rains Saturate Michigan appeared first on NASA Science.
